intro:
There is no information available on Lycopodioides compta. However, I can provide information on Lycopodium clavatum (Club Moss) which shares similarities with the name you provided and is a plant that is a member of the Lycopodiaceae family, a group of vascular, non-flowering plants.

Lycopodium clavatum is a type of club moss, a low-growing, evergreen plant native to the mountainous regions of the world. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lycopodium clavatum include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lycopodium clavatum include tinctures and capsules, which are used to treat respiratory issues and as an expectorant to help relieve coughs and congestion.
